theta0_small_angle_trig.description: Difference between mapped theta0 = -beta*sin(phi) and linearized theta0 = -beta*phi.

theta0p_radius_correction.description: Contribution from replacing x by deformed radius r in the curvature term of theta0'.

theta0p_sine_curvature_correction.description: Contribution from replacing phi by sin(phi) in the curvature term of theta0'.

theta0p_cosine_factor_correction.description: Contribution from the prefactor cos(phi) in theta0' = -beta*cos(phi)*kappa_s.

theta0p_combined_nonshallow_correction.description: Total gap between mapped theta0' and its small-angle / undeformed-radius linearization.

Phi0p_hoop_geometric_contribution.description: Contribution of the geometric hoop term ur/x inside Phi0' = gamma*(nu*T_s + ur/x).

hoop_strain_geometric_factor.description: Magnitude of the geometric hoop-strain factor ur/x entering T_theta.

ur_kinematic_cosine_gap.description: Kinematic gap in u_r' from the factor (1+e_s)*cos(phi) - 1 versus the linearized e_s.

uz_small_angle_trig_gap.description: Kinematic gap in u_z' from sin(phi) versus phi while keeping the same (1+e_s) prefactor.

interpretation.theta0: theta0 has a single direct mapped non-shallow correction in this pilot: the small-angle trig gap between sin(phi) and phi.
interpretation.theta0p: theta0' is decomposed additively into radius, sine-curvature, and cosine-prefactor corrections, plus their exact combined gap from the small-angle / undeformed-radius form.
interpretation.Phi0: Phi0 = gamma*x*T_s has no separate mapping-only non-shallow correction in the current comparison, so this pilot does not uniquely attribute Phi0 mismatch to a single term group.
interpretation.Phi0p: Phi0' is checked against the geometric hoop contribution gamma*(ur/x). This is informative for trend tracking but not a proof that the shallow model omits the same effect.
